hypothesis: Continual pre-training of a 2B instruct language model on MOF-specific literature and large PubChem chemical corpora, followed by supervised fine-tuning on MOFid-to-property datasets, will produce lower test MAE for band gap and gas adsorption predictions than CGCNN, SOAP, and MOFormer baselines and will yield larger relative performance gains in low-resource fine-tuning scenarios (1K–10K samples).
